sn38.12:1.1: "Friend Sāriputta, it is said, ‘clinging, clinging.’ What now is clinging?"
sn38.12:1.2: "There are, friend, these four kinds of clinging: clinging to sensual pleasures, clinging to views, clinging to rules and vows, clinging to a doctrine of self. These are the four kinds of clinging."
sn38.12:1.3: "But, friend, is there a path, is there a way for the abandonment of these four kinds of clinging?"
sn38.12:2.2: "There is a path, friend, there is a way … this Noble Eightfold Path … ."
